summary.intent: Implement Hiddify-style multi-profile (multi-subscription) VPN support in CyberVPN mobile app using a 5-agent team, allowing users to manage multiple VPN subscriptions with local Drift database storage, subscription URL parsing, profile switching, and auto-updates.
summary.outcome: Successfully implemented complete multi-profile feature across 29 tasks: Drift SQLite database with profiles/configs tables, domain entities (VpnProfile sealed class), repository layer, 4 UI screens with cyberpunk theme, subscription URL fetcher with HTTP header parsing, legacy migration integrated into router, 136 passing tests, and 5-tab navigation. All code committed and pushed.

repo: CyberVPN uses Riverpod 3.x with app-scoped StreamProviders for global state and autoDispose for screen-scoped state
Router-based initialization pattern: migration logic triggered via FutureProvider in GoRouter redirect guard, not in splash screen files directly
Existing codebase has separate user Profile (account) vs VPN profiles - required namespacing providers as vpnProfileRepositoryProvider
Cyberpunk theme tokens defined in lib/app/theme/tokens.dart: Orbitron for headings, JetBrains Mono for body, neon colors (matrixGreen, neonCyan, neonPink)
Config import system already had parsers for vmess/vless/trojan/shadowsocks URIs - reused for subscription URL parsing
path_provider ^2.1.6 doesn't exist, resolved to ^2.1.5 successfully

code.finding: Migration integrated via profileMigrationProvider read + isMigrationLoading flag in redirect guard - blocks navigation from splash until migration completes
Subscription URL headers: profile-title, subscription-userinfo (upload=X; download=Y; total=Z; expire=T format), profile-update-interval (hours), support-url - Base64 body decode to V2Ray URIs
Sealed union type VpnProfile.remote() and VpnProfile.local() - pattern matching via .when() for type-specific logic
Drift schemaVersion=1 with lazy singleton pattern, DB path at ${appDocDir}/cybervpn_profiles.db, field-level encryption for subscriptionUrl using SecureStorage master key
Decorator pattern for server list: watches activeVpnProfileProvider, delegates to backend API for CyberVPN native profiles, maps ProfileServer from Drift for third-party profiles

workflow: 5-agent parallel team execution: data-layer-dev + domain-dev started independent tasks (DL-1/DL-4 + DOM-1/DOM-2) immediately while ui-dev did i18n strings with no dependencies
Wave-based task assignment: Wave 1 (no deps) → Wave 2 (after DL-2, DOM-1) → Wave 3 (after DOM-3) maximized parallelism
data-layer-dev carried extra load: completed 9 tasks including DOM-3, DOM-5, TST-2 beyond their original 6 assignments
build_runner coordination: only one agent runs dart run build_runner at a time to prevent file lock conflicts
Agent idle notifications are normal between turns - not a blocker, just waiting for input
TeamCreate + TaskCreate/TaskUpdate for shared task list avoided bead conflicts with parallel agents
Riverpod 3.x API: class extends AsyncNotifier, provider uses AsyncNotifierProvider.autoDispose (not AutoDisposeAsyncNotifier class name)
flutter test hangs on full suite run - run profile-specific tests with --plain-name for faster feedback

friction: Initial assessment incorrectly concluded migration not wired (93% complete) - migration was integrated via router provider pattern, not splash file modification
flutter test full suite run hung - had to run profile-specific tests only
path_provider ^2.1.6 doesn't exist in pub.dev - auto-resolved to ^2.1.5
Multiple idle notifications from agents caused noise - normal Riverpod behavior but looked like blocking
Task status updates lagged behind actual completion - agents finished tasks before lead could update status
open_items: Phase B future work: Profile groups/folders, per-profile DNS settings, per-profile split tunnel rules
Phase C future work: Cloud sync via backend API (requires new endpoints), profile sharing/export
WireGuard/OpenVPN profile import not implemented - only V2Ray-based protocols (vmess, vless, trojan, shadowsocks)
Only app_en.arb has new i18n strings - other 26 locales will use English fallback until translated
Profile export/backup intentionally excluded from Android Auto Backup (subscription URLs are auth-scoped, not portable)
Manual config entry option in AddProfileScreen disabled (future enhancement)
Subscription URL fetch doesn't support proxy-through-connected-VPN yet (future: support network restrictions)
